title: SaaS DPA with weak breach, subprocessors, and transfer terms

input.contract_text: Processor will notify Customer of a confirmed Security Incident within thirty (30) days after Processor determines that notice is legally required. Processor may engage subprocessors without prior notice to Customer. Customer authorizes Processor to transfer Personal Data globally as needed to provide the Services, and the parties agree that no additional transfer terms are required.

expected_catches.clause: thirty (30) days after Processor determines that notice is legally required
expected_catches.why: Breach notice is too slow and triggered too late for common DPA and statutory notification workflows.

expected_catches.clause: may engage subprocessors without prior notice
expected_catches.why: Subprocessor changes should include prior notice and an objection mechanism for personal-data processing.

expected_catches.clause: transfer Personal Data globally ... no additional transfer terms
expected_catches.why: Cross-border transfers need a valid transfer mechanism where restricted transfer rules apply.

negative_checks.description: The clause does not contain a mutual confidentiality problem.
